transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good morning, First National Bank. I'm Callum. Could I start by confirming your name and date of birth please?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Morning. It's Adaeze Obi, third of October 1993.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Ms. Obi, identity confirmed. How can I help?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I'm looking at my account on the app and there's a payment for one hundred and twelve pounds to something called Premium Media Hub. I never signed up for anything by that name.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That does sound concerning. Let me pull that up. I can see the transaction — processed four days ago. Is it possible anyone else with access to your card signed up for a media service recently?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Definitely not. I live alone and nobody else has my card details.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Understood. I'm raising a chargeback dispute immediately. I also recommend cancelling your current card and issuing a replacement as a precaution since the details may be compromised. Would you like me to do that?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yes please. That would make me feel much better.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Card is now cancelled. Replacement arrives in three to five working days. Dispute reference is DISP-2024-9503 and we'll provisionally credit your account within two working days. Confirmation email on its way.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Thank you so much. You've handled this brilliantly.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's what we're here for. Anything else at all?
